summary: Bug-fix verification for user-confusion issue (admin creds attempted at /login instead of /admin/login). Backend behavior verified correct: POST /api/admin/login with 9999999999/Admin@12345 returns HTTP 200 with tokens.access_token and admin.role='admin'. POST /api/auth/login with same admin creds correctly returns HTTP 401 'Invalid mobile or password'. Regression: registered a fresh test user via full OTP flow (send-otp -> verify-otp -> register with state+city+referral RW000000 -> login) returned 200; BRV /api/admin/qa/brv still returns overall=PASS with 36/36 rules. Frontend: /login page shows new admin hint element [data-testid='login-admin-link'] with exact text 'Sign in to the admin panel →'; clicking it navigates to /admin/login; admin form fields fill and submit successfully; redirect to /admin/dashboard confirmed and 'RIYORA Admin' visible in AdminShell header; /admin/qa page still loads with admin-qa-page testid and 'Run BRV' still yields PASS 36/36 verdict card. All 7 test cases in the review request pass.
critical_code_review_comments: Login.jsx admin hint uses text-xs muted-foreground on white — legibility is OK on desktop but on mobile the muted color may fall below 4.5:1 WCAG contrast in some themes. Confirmed visually acceptable in the current tokens but worth verifying against production dark/high-contrast modes.

The Login.jsx registration payload requires state+city (server 422s otherwise), while /app/memory/test_credentials.md sample payload does NOT mention state/city. Consider updating the test-credentials doc so future test agents don't lose a cycle discovering the required fields.

success_rate.backend: 100% (3/3 targeted checks: admin login OK, user login rejected, BRV PASS 36/36, plus full user register+login regression 200)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (login-admin-link visible + navigates; admin login -> dashboard header 'RIYORA Admin'; /admin/qa Run BRV -> PASS 36/36)

test_credentials: Admin 9999999999 / Admin@12345 confirmed working via /api/admin/login. Fresh test user created: mobile 8700003095, password Passw0rd!, membership RW550573 (regression check only, not needed to preserve).
seed_data_creation: One TEST_BugFix User was created via /api/auth/register (mobile 8700003095, membership RW550573) for the user-login regression. No login_attempts rows created since no failed attempts were made during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug-fix (UX hint on /login pointing to /admin/login) is fully verified. Two-line change to Login.jsx confirmed live. No backend changes were required. If future testing hits an admin lockout on 9999999999, run: db.login_attempts.delete_one({'mobile':'9999999999','role':'admin'}). For fresh user registration, remember state and city are required fields in POST /api/auth/register (currently missing from test-credentials doc).
rca_of_the_issue: Not a code bug — the user typed admin credentials (9999999999 / Admin@12345) at /login (user endpoint) which correctly rejected them because admins live in a separate `admins` collection queried only by /api/admin/login. Two rejections created stale login_attempts rows that were cleared by the main agent. Fix is UX: added a subtle 'Administrator? Sign in to the admin panel →' link at the bottom of /login pointing to /admin/login with data-testid='login-admin-link'. Verified end-to-end.
